# SRE Report

## error_correction:competencies_api:8b0b5bfe242f
**Service**: competencies_api
**Original Error**: (sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql.asyncpg.Error) <class 'asyncpg.exceptions.DataError'>: invalid input for query argument $3: datetime.datetime(2026, 5, 6, 19, 12, 41... (can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes)
[SQL: INSERT INTO user_competency (user_id, competency_id, created_at) VALUES ($1::INTEGER, $2::INTEGER, $3::TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIME ZONE) ON CONFLICT (user_id, competency_id) DO NOTHING]
[parameters: (807, 1614, datetime.datetime(2026, 5, 6, 19, 12, 41, 494597, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))]
(Background on this error at: https://sqlalche.me/e/20/dbapi)
**Rule**: NEVER pass timezone-aware datetime strings to the `created_at` parameter in `competencies_api` tools. The database column `user_competency.created_at` is offset-naive and cannot handle timezone information, causing a `DataError`.
❌ `add_competency_to_user(user_id=807, competency_id=1614, created_at='2026-05-06T19:12:41Z')`
✅ `add_competency_to_user(user_id=807, competency_id=1614, created_at='2026-05-06 19:12:41')`
If this `DBAPIError` or `DataError` recurs, use `get_service_logs` for `competencies_api` to investigate the input parameters.

[INVESTIGATION REQUISE] En cas d'erreur similaire, vous DEVEZ rechercher les logs dans Cloud Logging pour le service 'competencies_api' sur le projet GCP 'prod-ia-staffing'.

        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

**Original Error**: (sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql.asyncpg.Error) <class 'asyncpg.exceptions.DataError'>: invalid input for query argument $3: datetime.datetime(2026, 5, 6, 19, 13, 4,... (can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes)
[SQL: INSERT INTO user_competency (user_id, competency_id, created_at) VALUES ($1::INTEGER, $2::INTEGER, $3::TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIME ZONE) ON CONFLICT (user_id, competency_id) DO NOTHING]
[parameters: (473, 82, datetime.datetime(2026, 5, 6, 19, 13, 4, 438979, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))]
(Background on this error at: https://sqlalche.me/e/20/dbapi)
**Rule**: NEVER pass timezone-aware datetime strings to the `created_at` parameter of any `competencies_api` tool. The database requires a naive timestamp without any timezone information (e.g., 'Z' or '+00:00').
❌ `add_user_competency(user_id=473, competency_id=82, created_at='2026-05-06T19:13:04Z')`
❌ `add_user_competency(user_id=473, competency_id=82, created_at='2026-05-06T19:13:04+00:00')`
✅ `add_user_competency(user_id=473, competency_id=82, created_at='2026-05-06T19:13:04')`
If a `DBAPIError` related to datetimes recurs, use `get_service_logs` for `competencies_api` to investigate the exact parameters passed.

    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
        self._prepare_and_execute(operation, parameters)
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    )
    ^

           ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^

    value = await result
            ^^^^^^^^^^^^

    self._handle_exception(error)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^

**Original Error**: (sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql.asyncpg.Error) <class 'asyncpg.exceptions.DataError'>: invalid input for query argument $3: datetime.datetime(2026, 5, 6, 19, 13, 39... (can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes)
[SQL: INSERT INTO user_competency (user_id, competency_id, created_at) VALUES ($1::INTEGER, $2::INTEGER, $3::TIMESTAMP WITHOUT TIME ZONE) ON CONFLICT (user_id, competency_id) DO NOTHING]
[parameters: (685, 5210, datetime.datetime(2026, 5, 6, 19, 13, 39, 901796, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc))]
(Background on this error at: https://sqlalche.me/e/20/dbapi)
**Rule**: NEVER pass timezone-aware datetime objects to any `competencies_api` tool. The database schema requires timezone-naive timestamps for fields like `created_at`, and passing offset-aware objects causes a `sqlalchemy.exc.DBAPIError`.
❌ `tool: competencies_api.add_user_competency(user_id=685, competency_id=5210, created_at=datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc))`
✅ `tool: competencies_api.add_user_competency(user_id=685, competency_id=5210, created_at=datetime.datetime.utcnow())`
If this `DataError` recurs, immediately use `get_service_logs` for the `competencies_api` to investigate the exact parameter mismatch.

        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

    ^

            ^^^^^^^^^^^^
